QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_58

question.text: Which of the following details must be included in the post-issue advertisement?

answer.text: Names of all applicants who were allotted shares
Details of the underwriting agreements signed
Subscription details, basis of allotment, number of ASBA applications, dates of refund/credit of shares/listing
Detailed financial projections of the issuer company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The post-issue advertisement must contain subscription details, basis of allotment, number of ASBA applications received and processed, dates of refund, credit of securities to demat accounts, and listing dates. It must also be displayed on stock exchange websites. **The Trap:** Names of individual allottees are never published in advertisements — that would violate privacy. Financial projections belong in the offer document, not in the post-issue advertisement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Post-issue disclosure requirements are primarily tested in NISM Series IX. NISM Series XII (Securities Markets Foundation) covers basic listing requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The post-issue advertisement serves as a comprehensive summary for applicants to track their application status — refund dates and credit dates are the most closely watched by retail investors.
